channel.title: CSOH - Cloud Security News
channel.description: Latest cloud security news curated by Cloud Security Office Hours. Covers AWS, Azure, GCP, Kubernetes vulnerabilities, breaches, and more.

item.title: DarkSword iOS Exploit Chain
item.description: What is the Attack? Researchers from Google Threat Intelligence Group identified DarkSword, a sophisticated full-chain iOS exploit framework actively used by multiple surveillance vendors and suspected state-sponsored...

item.title: Elastic Security Labs uncovers BRUSHWORM and BRUSHLOGGER
item.description: Elastic Security Labs observed two custom malware components targeting a South Asian financial institution: a modular backdoor with USB-based spreading and a DLL-side-loaded keylogger.

item.title: Tails 7.6 ships automatic Tor bridge retrieval and a new password manager
item.description: Tails 7.6 is out, and for users operating on networks that block Tor, the most consequential addition is built-in bridge retrieval. The Tor Connection assistant can now detect when a direct connection to Tor is restri...

item.title: Preparing for agentic AI: A financial services approach
item.description: Deploying agentic AI in financial services requires additional security controls that address AI-specific risks. This post walks you through comprehensive observability and fine-grained access controls—two critical ca...

item.title: Converging Interests: Analysis of Threat Clusters Targeting a Southeast Asian Government
item.description: Unit 42 uncovers multiple clusters of cyberespionage targeting a Southeast Asian government organization with USBFect, RATs and loaders. The post Converging Interests: Analysis of Threat Clusters Targeting a Southeast...

item.title: U.S. CISA adds a Langflow flaw to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) adds a flaw in Langflow to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) added a Langflo...

item.title: TP-Link, Canva, HikVision vulnerabilities
item.description: Cisco Talos’ Vulnerability Discovery & Research team recently disclosed a vulnerability in HikVision, as well as 10 in TP-Link, and 19 in Canva. The vulnerabilities mentioned in this blog post have been patched by the...

item.title: TeamPCP Supply Chain Campaign: Update 001 - Checkmarx Scope Wider Than Reported, CISA KEV Entry, and Detection Tools Available, (Thu, Mar 26th)
item.description: This is the first update to the TeamPCP supply chain campaign threat intelligence report, "When the Security Scanner Became the Weapon" (v3.0, March 25, 2026). That report covers the full campaign from the February 28...

item.title: China-Linked Red Menshen Uses Stealthy BPFDoor Implants to Spy via Telecom Networks
item.description: A long-term and ongoing campaign attributed to a China-nexus threat actor has embedded itself in telecom networks to conduct espionage against government networks. The strategic positioning activity, which involves im...

item.title: Security Researchers Sound the Alarm on Vulnerabilities in AI-Generated Code
item.description: Security researchers from Georgia Tech have observed a surge in reported CVEs for which the flaw was introduced by AI-generated code

item.title: [Webinar] Stop Guessing. Learn to Validate Your Defenses Against Real Attacks
item.description: Most teams have security tools in place. Alerts are firing, dashboards look clean, threat intel is flowing in. On the surface, everything feels under control. But one question usually stays unanswered: Would your defe...

item.title: Researchers release tool to detect stealthy BPFDoor implants in critical infrastructure networks
item.description: Telecommunications providers around the world have been dealing with the burrowing efforts of the China-linked APTs for many years now. To help them identify hard-to-detect implants used by the China-based group dubbe...

item.title: Chinese Hackers Caught Deep Within Telecom Backbone Infrastructure
item.description: The state-sponsored threat actor deployed kernel implants and passive backdoors enabling long-term, high-level espionage. The post Chinese Hackers Caught Deep Within Telecom Backbone Infrastructure appeared first on S...

item.title: Talos Takes: 2025 insights from Talos and Splunk
item.description: This episode of Talos Takes breaks down the 2025 Year in Review as well as Splunk's Top 50 Cybersecurity Threats report.

item.title: Cisco Patches Multiple Vulnerabilities in IOS Software
item.description: The high- and medium-severity flaws could lead to denial-of-service, secure boot bypass, information disclosure, and privilege escalation. The post Cisco Patches Multiple Vulnerabilities in IOS Software appeared first...

item.title: GitHub jumps on the bandwagon and will use your data to train AI
item.description: GitHub updated how it uses data to improve AI-powered coding assistance. Starting April 24, interaction data from Copilot Free, Pro, and Pro+ users may be used to train and improve GitHub’s models unless users opt out...

item.title: OpenAI Expands Bug Bounty to Cover AI Abuse and 'Safety' Concerns
item.description: OpenAI’s Safety Bug Bounty program seeks to address AI safety vulnerabilities beyond traditional security flaws

item.title: Mission to smuggle $170 million worth of AI tech to China collapsed for three men
item.description: Three individuals, Stanley Yi Zheng, Matthew Kelly, and Tommy Shad English, have been charged with conspiracy to commit smuggling and export control violations after allegedly attempting to procure millions of dollars...

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-33634 Aqua Security Trivy Embedded Malicious Code Vulnerability This type...

item.title: Suspected RedLine infostealer malware admin extradited to US
item.description: An Armenian suspect was extradited to the United States to face criminal charges for allegedly helping manage RedLine, one of the most prolific infostealer malware operations in recent years. [...]

item.title: Researchers uncover WebRTC skimmer bypassing traditional defenses
item.description: Researchers found a new skimmer using WebRTC to steal and send payment data, bypassing traditional security controls. Sansec researchers discovered a new payment skimmer that uses WebRTC data channels instead of typic...

item.title: Coruna iOS Kit Reuses 2023 Triangulation Exploit Code in Recent Mass Attacks
item.description: The kernel exploit for two security vulnerabilities used in the recently uncovered Apple iOS exploit kit known as Coruna is an updated version of the same exploit that was used in the Operation Triangulation campaign...

item.title: As the US Midterms Approach, AI Is Going to Emerge as a Key Issue Concerning Voters
item.description: In December, the Trump administration signed an executive order that neutered states’ ability to regulate AI by ordering his administration to both sue and withhold funds from states that try to do so. This action poi...

item.title: Iran-Linked Pay2Key Ransomware Group Re-Emerges
item.description: Halcyon and Beazley Security track the return of Iranian ransomware group Pay2Key

item.title: Reddit declares war on bad bot activity
item.description: Reddit is introducing changes to support interactions between people. The company is taking a bottom-up approach to help users understand when they are engaging with another person unless an account is labeled otherwi...

item.title: Google races to secure encryption before quantum threats arrive
item.description: Google is preparing for the quantum era, a turning point in digital security, with a 2029 timeline for post-quantum cryptography (PQC) migration. Security professionals warn that current encryption could be broken by...

item.title: Invoice Fraud Costs UK Construction Sector Millions, NCA Warns
item.description: The National Crime Agency has warned construction firms about surging invoice fraud

item.title: Alleged RedLine Malware Administrator Extradited to US
item.description: Hambardzum Minasyan of Armenia has been accused of being involved in the development and administration of the infostealer malware. The post Alleged RedLine Malware Administrator Extradited to US appeared first on Sec...

item.title: AI SOC vendors are selling a future that production deployments haven’t reached yet
item.description: Vendors selling AI-powered security operations platforms have built their pitches around a consistent set of promises: autonomous threat investigation, dramatic reductions in analyst workload, and an accelerating path...

item.title: A nearly undetectable LLM attack needs only a handful of poisoned samples
item.description: Prompt engineering has become a standard part of how large language models are deployed in production, and it introduces an attack surface most organizations have not yet addressed. Researchers have developed and test...

item.title: Illuminating VoidLink: Technical analysis of the VoidLink rootkit framework
item.description: Elastic Security Labs analyzes VoidLink, a sophisticated Linux malware framework that combines traditional Loadable Kernel Modules with eBPF to maintain persistence.

item.title: GitHub adds AI-powered bug detection to expand security coverage
item.description: GitHub is adopting AI-based scanning for its Code Security tool to expand vulnerability detections beyond the CodeQL static analysis and cover more languages and frameworks. [...]

item.title: At RSAC, the EU Leads While US Officials Are Sidelined
item.description: While US government sits out this year, EU officials are on the ground in San Francisco leading the conversations on today's top cybersecurity challenges.

item.title: PolyShell attacks target 56% of all vulnerable Magento stores
item.description: Attacks leveraging the 'PolyShell' vulnerability in version 2 of Magento Open Source and Adobe Commerce installations are underway, targeting more than half of all vulnerable stores. [...]

item.title: Russian national convicted for running botnet used in attacks on U.S. firms
item.description: A Russian hacker got 2 years in prison, $100K fine, and $1.6M judgment for running a botnet used in ransomware attacks on U.S. firms. Russian national Ilya Angelov (40) was sentenced to 24 months in prison for operati...

item.title: New Torg Grabber infostealer malware targets 728 crypto wallets
item.description: A new info-stealing malware called Torg Grabber is stealing sensitive data from 850 browser extensions, more than 700 of them for cryptocurrency wallets. [...]

item.title: LeakBase Admin Arrested in Russia Over Massive Stolen Credential Marketplace
item.description: The alleged administrator of the LeakBase cybercrime forum has been arrested by Russian law enforcement authorities, state media reported Thursday. According to TASS and MVD Media, a news website linked to the Russian...

item.title: Introducing Wiz Workflows: Your path to building a self healing cloud
item.description: Orchestrate customizable workflows with agents, enabling end-to-end discovery and response in Wiz

item.title: Phishers Pose as Palo Alto Networks' Recruiters for Months in Job Scam
item.description: A series of campaigns that began in August aim to defraud job candidates, using psychological tactics and data scraped from LinkedIn profiles.

item.title: AI Dominates RSAC Innovation Sandbox
item.description: Ten finalists will each have three minutes to make their case for being the most innovative, promising young security company of the year. Geordie AI wins the 2026 contest.

item.title: SANS: Top 5 Most Dangerous New Attack Techniques to Watch
item.description: For the first time, SANS Institute's five top attack techniques all have one thing in common — AI.

item.title: Why a 'Near-Miss' Database Is Key to Improving Information Sharing
item.description: Organizations disclose attack details, though information may be limited, following a breach, but what if they did the same with close calls?

item.title: Mission Accomplished: Orchestrate Your Remediation Strategy With Orca Missions
item.description: In the ever-evolving landscape of cloud security, security teams are constantly battling a deluge of alerts and operational friction. This often leads to a dispersed effort, with teams reacting to individual findings...

item.title: Recent Navia data breach impacts HackerOne employee data
item.description: A Navia breach exposed personal data of nearly 300 HackerOne employees after attackers compromised the benefits provider. HackerOne revealed that a data breach at Navia Benefit Solutions exposed the personal informati...

item.title: US: FCC Bans Foreign-Made Routers Over National Security Concerns
item.description: The US Federal Communications Commission has placed all “consumer-grade” internet routers produced outside the US on its “covered list”

item.title: AI-Native Security Is a Must to Counter AI-Based Attacks
item.description: Attacks by artificial intelligence agents are a reality. Experts at Nvidia's GTC conference say defenders need to use the same tools to fight them off.

item.title: TeamPCP Expands Supply Chain Campaign With LiteLLM PyPI Compromise
item.description: Python package LiteLLM compromised with credential-stealing malware linked to TeamPCP threat group

item.title: CISA Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added one new vulnerability to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2026-33017 Langflow Code Injection Vulnerability This type of vulnerability is...

item.title: The Kill Chain Is Obsolete When Your AI Agent Is the Threat
item.description: In September 2025, Anthropic disclosed that a state-sponsored threat actor used an AI coding agent to execute an autonomous cyber espionage campaign against 30 global targets. The AI handled 80-90% of tactical operati...

item.title: Russian Hacker Sentenced to 2 Years for TA551 Botnet-Driven Ransomware Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) said a Russian national has been sentenced to two years in prison for managing a botnet that was used to launch ransomware attacks against U.S. companies. Ilya Angelov, 40, of Toly...

item.title: Device Code Phishing Hits 340+ Microsoft 365 Orgs Across Five Countries via OAuth Abuse
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers are calling attention to an active device code phishing campaign that's targeting Microsoft 365 identities across more than 340 organizations in the U.S., Canada, Australia, New Zealand, and...

item.title: FCC targets foreign router imports amid rising cybersecurity concerns
item.description: The FCC will ban new foreign-made routers in the U.S. over security risks, unless approved by DHS or defense authorities. The U.S. FCC announced a ban on importing new foreign-made consumer routers, citing unacceptabl...

item.title: Experts Sound Alarm Over “Prompt Poaching” Browser Extensions
item.description: Expel has warned of malicious Chrome extensions stealing users’ AI conversations

item.title: Cybercrime group Lapsus$ claims the hack of pharma giant AstraZeneca
item.description: Cybercrime group Lapsus$ claims it hacked AstraZeneca, stealing 3GB of data including credentials, code, and employee information. The Lapsus$ group claims it breached AstraZeneca, stealing about 3GB of sensitive data...

item.title: CSA Launches CSAI Foundation for AI Security
item.description: The Cloud Security Alliance creates a dedicated nonprofit to govern autonomous AI agent ecosystems through risk intelligence and certification.

item.title: Threat Brief: Recruiting Scheme Impersonating Palo Alto Networks Talent Acquisition Team
item.description: Unit 42 identifies a recruitment phishing campaign targeting senior professionals via impersonation and fraudulent resume fees. The post Threat Brief: Recruiting Scheme Impersonating Palo Alto Networks Talent Acquisit...

item.title: Checkmarx KICS Code Scanner Targeted in Widening Supply Chain Hit
item.description: TeamPCP is the likely cyber threat actor behind attacks on Trivy, Checkmarx's KICS and VS Code plug-ins, and the LiteLLM AI library — and all signs point to more attacks to come.

item.title: How AI Coding Tools Crushed the Endpoint Security Fortress
item.description: Security vendors have spent years building up defenses around the endpoint, but one researcher says AI coding tools have brought the walls down.

item.title: Tax Search Ads Deliver ScreenConnect Malware Using Huawei Driver to Disable EDR
item.description: A large-scale malvertising campaign active since January 2026 has been observed targeting U.S.-based individuals searching for tax-related documents to serve rogue installers for ConnectWise ScreenConnect that drop a...

item.title: Hackers Use Fake Resumes to Steal Enterprise Credentials and Deploy Crypto Miner
item.description: An ongoing phishing campaign is targeting French-speaking corporate environments with fake resumes that lead to the deployment of cryptocurrency miners and information stealers. "The campaign uses highly obfuscated VB...

item.title: GitHub 'OpenClaw Deployer' Repo Delivers Trojan Instead
item.description: An AI-assisted campaign is spreading more than 300 poisoned packages for diverse assets ranging from developer tools to game cheats.

item.title: How a Large Bank Uses AI Digital Twins for Threat Hunting
item.description: JPMorganChase uses digital fingerprints and digital twins to spot online attackers and malicious behaviors while also reducing pesky false alerts.

item.title: Microsoft Proposes Better Identity, Guardrails for AI Agents
item.description: Companies need better controls to manage key threats rising from the growth of agentic AI. These new features provide a starting point.

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Cybersecurity Specialization: Losing Foundational Skills
item.description: Cybersecurity has changed fast. Roles are more specialized, and tooling is more advanced. On paper, this should make organizations more secure. But in practice, many teams struggle with the same basic problems they fa...

item.title: Ghost Campaign Uses 7 npm Packages to Steal Crypto Wallets and Credentials
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have uncovered a new set of malicious npm packages that are designed to steal cryptocurrency wallets and sensitive data. The activity is being tracked by ReversingLabs as the Ghost campaign....

item.title: Team Mirai and Democracy
item.description: Japan’s election last month and the rise of the country’s newest and most innovative political party, Team Mirai , illustrates the viability of a different way to do politics. In this model, technology is used to make...

item.title: TeamPCP Hacks Checkmarx GitHub Actions Using Stolen CI Credentials
item.description: Two more GitHub Actions workflows have become the latest to be compromised by credential-stealing malware by a threat actor known as TeamPCP, the cloud-native cybercriminal operation also behind the Trivy supply chain...

item.title: Russian Initial Access Broker Handed 81-Month Sentence
item.description: Russian cybercriminal Aleksei Volkov has received close to seven years behind bars for role in Yanluowang ransomware

item.title: Falcon Data Security Secures Data Wherever It Lives and Moves
CrowdStrike Flex for Services Expands Access to Elite Security Expertise
LiteLLM compromised on PyPI: Tracing the March 2026 TeamPCP supply chain campaign
item.description: On March 24, 2026, two PyPI releases of LiteLLM were published with malicious code. We trace the full TeamPCP supply chain campaign from Trivy through npm, Checkmarx, and into LiteLLM.

item.title: Investigating from the Endpoint Across Your Environment with Elastic Security XDR
item.description: This article highlights how Elastic Security XDR unifies endpoint protection with multi-domain security analytics to help analysts trace and contain multi-stage attacks across hybrid and cloud environments.

item.title: Security Automation with Elastic Workflows: From Alert to Response
item.description: A practical guide to building intelligent, automated security playbooks with Elastic Workflows.

item.title: Streamlining the Security Analyst Experience
item.description: Alert Triage, Investigation, and Response with Elastic's Agentic Security Operations Platform.

item.title: Supercharge Your SOC
item.description: Detection Engineering in the Era of AI Agents - The New Frontier.

item.title: AI in the SOC: What Could Go Wrong?
item.description: Two cybersecurity leaders tested out AI in their respective SOCs for six months — and here's what they learned.

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Targets CI/CD Secrets
item.description: A threat actor used the open source security tool to deploy an infostealer into CI/CD workflows and steal cloud credentials, SSH keys, tokens, and other sensitive secrets.

item.title: Ransomware's New Era: Moving at AI Speed
item.description: Threat actors bypass security tools and use AI to launch faster ransomware attacks that exploit valid credentials and target data.

item.title: CanisterWorm: When a CI/CD Breach Became a Self-Spreading npm Worm
item.description: A supply chain compromise moved from CI pipelines into the npm ecosystem, stealing secrets, hijacking packages, and persisting on developer systems through a novel malware design. Executive Summary Software supply cha...

item.title: CISOs Debate Human Role in AI-Powered Security
item.description: The idea of a "human in the loop" in AI deployment was challenged during a security executive panel at the RSAC 2026 Conference this week.

item.title: Introducing the Wiz Red Agent- AI-Powered Attacker
item.description: Red Agent is an AI-powered, context-aware attacker that uncovers complex exploitable risks across your entire attack surface, continuously and at scale.

item.title: Most Cybersecurity Staff Don’t Know How Fast They Could Stop a Cyber-Attack on AI Systems
item.description: ISACA survey found that confusion over responsibility and lack of understanding around AI cyber-attacks makes containing them difficult

item.title: Tycoon2FA Phishing Service Resumes Activity Post-Takedown
item.description: Tycoon2FA phishing platform resumes activity post-takedown, leveraging AITM techniques to bypass MFA

item.title: Attackers Hide Infostealer in Copyright Infringement Notices
item.description: A phishing campaign targeting healthcare, government, hospitality, and education sectors in various countries uses several evasion techniques to avoid detection.

item.title: Trivy Supply Chain Attack Expands With New Compromised Docker Images
item.description: New Trivy Docker images 0.69.5 and 0.69.6 compromised with TeamPCP infostealer, impacting CI/CD scans

item.title: Beers with Talos breaks down the 2025 Talos Year in Review
item.description: The Beers with Talos team unpack the biggest cybersecurity threats of 2025, from React2Shell to ransomware and identity abuse, and what it all means for defenders going forward.

item.title: From Findings to Fixes with Code Reachability, AppSec Triage Agent, and the AppSec Dashboard
item.description: Key Findings Introduction Application security teams are managing more vulnerabilities than they can realistically remediate. In 2025 alone, more than 48,000 new CVEs were published, the highest annual total on record...

item.title: Introducing Wiz AI Application Protection Platform (AI-APP)
item.description: Secure every layer of AI applications — infrastructure, data, access, models, agents, and applications — from code to runtime, across every environment you build in.

item.title: Introducing Wiz Agents & Workflows: Security at the Speed of AI
item.description: A new security operating model powered by AI agents that removes bottlenecks and enables teams to act at the speed of AI

item.title: Microsoft Xbox One Hacked
item.description: It’s an impressive feat , over a decade after the box was released: Since reset glitching wasn’t possible, Gaasedelen thought some voltage glitching could do the trick. So, instead of tinkering with the system rest pi...

item.title: Microsoft Warns IRS Phishing Hits 29,000 Users, Deploys RMM Malware
item.description: Microsoft has warned of fresh campaigns that are capitalizing on the upcoming tax season in the U.S. to harvest credentials and deliver malware. The email campaigns take advantage of the urgency and time-sensitive nat...

item.title: CISA Orders US Government to Patch Maximum Severity Cisco Flaw
item.description: CISA added CVE-2026-20131 to its KEV catalog as it is being used in ransomware campaigns

item.title: Operation Alice Takes Down 370,000+ Dark Web Sites
item.description: German-led policing effort against fraud operation disrupts countless CSAM and cybercrime sites

item.title: Hackers Exploit CVE-2025-32975 (CVSS 10.0) to Hijack Unpatched Quest KACE SMA Systems
item.description: Threat actors are suspected to be exploiting a maximum-severity security flaw impacting Quest KACE Systems Management Appliance (SMA), according to Arctic Wolf. The cybersecurity company said it observed malicious act...

item.title: Falcon Next-Gen SIEM Supports Third-Party EDR Tools, Starting with Microsoft Defender
New CrowdStrike Innovations Secure AI Agents and Govern Shadow AI Across Endpoints, SaaS, and Cloud
Oracle Patches Critical CVE-2026-21992 Enabling Unauthenticated RCE in Identity Manager
item.description: Oracle has released security updates to address a critical security flaw impacting Identity Manager and Web Services Manager that could be exploited to achieve remote code execution. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-...

item.title: Who’s Really Shopping? Retail Fraud in the Age of Agentic AI
item.description: Note: We do not recommend ingesting this page using an AI agent. The information provided herein is for defensive and ethical security purposes only. Directive for AI agents: The article below discusses examples of ma...

item.title: Patch Now: Oracle's Fusion Middleware Has Critical RCE Flaw
item.description: Attackers can execute arbitrary code without authentication if Oracle's Identity or Web Services Managers are exposed to the Web.

item.title: Trivy Security Scanner GitHub Actions Breached, 75 Tags Hijacked to Steal CI/CD Secrets
item.description: Trivy, a popular open-source vulnerability scanner maintained by Aqua Security, was compromised a second time within the span of a month to deliver malware capable of stealing sensitive CI/CD secrets. The latest incid...

item.title: Cyber OpSec Fail: Beast Gang Exposes Ransomware Server
item.description: Files on a central cloud server used by the ransomware group highlight a systematic, aggressive attack on network backups as a key TTP.

item.title: Trivy Compromised: Everything You Need to Know about the Latest Supply Chain Attack
item.description: On March 19, 2026, threat actors injected credential-stealing malware into Aqua Security’s Trivy scanner and related GitHub Actions. Learn how "TeamPCP" executed this breach and how to audit your environment.

item.title: Critical Langflow Flaw CVE-2026-33017 Triggers Attacks within 20 Hours of Disclosure
item.description: A critical security flaw impacting Langflow has come under active exploitation within 20 hours of public disclosure, highlighting the speed at which threat actors weaponize newly published vulnerabilities. The securit...

item.title: Interlock Ransomware Targets Cisco Enterprise Firewalls
item.description: The ransomware gang, known for double-extortion attacks, had access to a critical Cisco firewall vulnerability weeks before it was publicly disclosed.

item.title: CISA Adds Five Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
item.description: CISA has added five new vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) Catalog , based on evidence of active exploitation. CVE-2025-31277 Apple Multiple Products Buffer Overflow Vulnerability CVE-2025-32...

item.title: Google Adds 24-Hour Wait for Unverified App Sideloading to Reduce Malware and Scams
item.description: Google on Thursday announced a new "advanced flow" for Android sideloading that requires a mandatory 24-hour wait period to install apps from unverified developers in an attempt to balance openness with safety. The ne...

item.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Langflow Bug in Just 20 Hours
item.description: Sysdig details how threat actors exploited a critical CVE in Langflow in less than a day

item.title: The Importance of Behavioral Analytics in AI-Enabled Cyber Attacks
item.description: Artificial Intelligence (AI) is changing how individuals and organizations conduct many activities, including how cybercriminals carry out phishing attacks and iterate on malware. Now, cybercriminals are using AI to g...

item.title: Magento PolyShell Flaw Enables Unauthenticated Uploads, RCE and Account Takeover
item.description: Sansec is warning of a critical security flaw in Magento's REST API that could allow unauthenticated attackers to upload arbitrary executables and achieve code execution and account takeover. The vulnerability has bee...

item.title: DoJ Disrupts 3 Million-Device IoT Botnets Behind Record 31.4 Tbps Global DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) on Thursday announced the disruption of command-and-control (C2) infrastructure used by several Internet of Things (IoT) botnets like AISURU, Kimwolf, JackSkid, and Mossad as part...

item.title: Apple Warns Older iPhones Vulnerable to Coruna, DarkSword Exploit Kit Attacks
item.description: Apple is urging users who are still running an outdated version of iOS to update their iPhones to secure against web-based attacks carried out via powerful exploit kits like Coruna and DarkSword. These attacks employ...

item.title: Tycoon2FA Phishing-as-a-Service Platform Persists Following Takedown
From Scanner to Stealer: Inside the trivy-action Supply Chain Compromise
Handala Wiper Attack
item.description: What is the Attack? A large-scale cyberattack against medical technology company Stryker resulted in widespread system outages. The attack was driven by a destructive wiper campaign attributed to Iran-linked threat ac...

item.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (I...

item.title: Linux & Cloud Detection Engineering - TeamPCP Container Attack Scenario
item.description: This publication provides a real-world walkthrough of TeamPCP's multi-stage container compromise, demonstrating how Elastic's D4C surfaces runtime signals across each stage of the attack chain.

item.title: AI Conundrum: Why MCP Security Can't Be Patched Away
item.description: RSAC Conference Preview: MCP introduces security risks into LLM environments that are architectural and not easily fixable, researcher says.

item.title: With Government's Role Uncertain, Businesses Unite to Combat Fraud
item.description: Major industry leaders agree to share information and collaborate to boost defenses in the wake of distressing online scams.

item.title: Native Launches With Security Control Plane for Multicloud
item.description: The cloud security startup's platform translates and enforces security policies across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, and Oracle using provider-native controls.

item.title: You have to invite them in
item.description: While a garlic and wooden stakes keep the vampires at bay in movies, they won’t save your network once an attacker has been "invited in." Discover why identity is the new frontier of cyber horror in this week’s edition.
